meta.description: Best practices for building Next.js (App Router) apps with modern caching, tooling, and server/client boundaries (aligned with Next.js 16.1.1). Triggers on: **/*.tsx, **/*.ts, **/*.jsx, **/*.js, **/*.

original_definition.skill_description: Best practices for building Next.js (App Router) apps with modern caching, tooling, and server/client boundaries (aligned with Next.js 16.1.1). Triggers on: **/*.tsx, **/*.ts, **/*.jsx, **/*.js, **/*.

actions.description: Private Folders: Prefix with `_` (e.g., `_internal`) to opt out of routing and signal implementation details.

actions.description: 2. Import and use that Client Component directly in the Server Component (no need for `next/dynamic`).

actions.description: 3. If you need to compose multiple client-only elements (e.g., a navbar with a profile dropdown), create a single Client Component that contains all o

actions.name: Server Components cannot use client-only features or dynamic
actions.description: Server Components cannot use client-only features or dynamic imports with SSR disabled.

actions.name: Client Components can be rendered inside Server Components,
actions.description: Client Components can be rendered inside Server Components, but not the other way around.

actions.name: HTTP Methods: Export async functions named after HTTP verbs
actions.description: HTTP Methods: Export async functions named after HTTP verbs (`GET`, `POST`, etc.).

actions.name: Use `cacheLife(...)` to control cache lifetime (presets or c
actions.description: Use `cacheLife(...)` to control cache lifetime (presets or configured profiles).

actions.description: Use `updateTag(...)` inside Server Actions when you need “read-your-writes” / immediate consistency.

actions.description: Use the following tools to fetch and search documentation if they are available:

actions.description: `resolve_library_id` to resolve the package/library name in the docs.

rules.description: Colocation: Place files (components, styles, tests) near where they are used, but avoid deeply nested structures.
Never use `next/dynamic` with `{ ssr: false }` inside a Server Component. This is not supported and will cause a build/runtime error.
rules.condition: 2.1. server and client component integration (app router)

rules.description: If you need to use a Client Component (e.g., a component that uses hooks, browser APIs, or client-only libraries) inside a Server Component, you must:
rules.condition: 2.1. server and client component integration (app router)

rules.description: Always move client-only UI into a Client Component and import it directly in your Server Component. Never use `next/dynamic` with `{ ssr: false }` in
rules.condition: 2.1. server and client component integration (app router)

rules.description: Avoid dynamic rendering by accident: Accessing request data (cookies/headers/searchParams) opts the route into dynamic behavior. Read them intentional
Validation: Always validate and sanitize input. Use libraries like `zod` or `yup`.
Authentication: Protect sensitive routes using middleware or server-side session checks.
Do not call your own Route Handlers from Server Components (e.g., `fetch('/api/...')`) just to reuse logic. Prefer extracting shared logic into module

directives.description: This document summarizes the latest, authoritative best practices for building, structuring, and maintaining Next.js applications. It is intended for
Use the `app/` directory (App Router) for all new projects. Prefer it over the legacy `pages/` directory.
`app/` — Routing, layouts, pages, and route handlers
Route Groups: Use parentheses (e.g., `(admin)`) to group routes without affecting the URL path.
Feature Folders: For large apps, group by feature (e.g., `app/dashboard/`, `app/auth/`).
Use `src/` (optional): Place all source code in `src/` to separate from config files.
1. Move all client-only logic/UI into a dedicated Client Component (with `'use client'` at the top).
Assume request-bound data is async in Server Components and Route Handlers. In Next.js 16, APIs like `cookies()`, `headers()`, and `draftMode()` are a
